title: Implement Queue Using Array
description: Design and implement a queue using an array. The queue should support the following operations: enqueue, dequeue, peek, and checking if the queue is empty. Note that the queue should have a fixed size that cannot be exceeded.

examples.input: Queue q = new Queue(5); q.enqueue(1); q.enqueue(2); q.enqueue(3);
examples.explanation: After enqueuing 1, 2, and 3, calling peek returns 1 because it is the first element in the queue.
After dequeuing, the queue removes the first element (1), so the next element to be returned by peek is now 2.
The initial size of the queue is 5. After enqueuing four elements (1, 2, 3, 4, and 5), attempting to add 6 or 7 will result in an error as the queue has reached its limit.

constraints: The maximum size of the queue will not exceed 1000.
All enqueue operations will be valid unless the queue is full.
hints: Consider using two pointers to track the front and rear of the queue.
Think about how to handle wrap-around when the queue reaches the end of the array.
Make sure to handle edge cases like dequeueing from an empty queue.
You might want to use modulo operation for circular behavior.
